gap.title: Talented, but invisible online?
gap.intro: You're booked on referrals and reputation. But new clients can't find you, can't tell you're premium, and can't book without sliding into your DMs. That's not a talent problem. It's a brand problem — and brand problems are fixable.
now: A blurry Linktree doing a website's job
New clients who can only reach you by DM
A feed that looks nothing like how good you are
Charging less than the work is worth
after: A booking-ready website that works the front desk for you
A brand that reads premium before you say a word
Short-form content that pulls appointments, not just likes
The look — and the nerve — to raise your rates
First in line when your city searches "near me"

packages.title: Choose your glow-up
packages.intro: Three ways to work together — a quick polish, a full identity, or a total takeover. Every one is built to do a single thing: Keep the chair full.
packages.retainerNote: Want the chair full every month, not just at launch? Ask about The Chair-Filler — monthly content, newsletters, and updates from $750/mo.

consults.title: Rather do it yourself?
consults.intro: Book my brain, not my whole studio. Deep strategy sessions for salons who want to build it themselves but need the map before they start. Your fee credits toward a full package if you upgrade within 30 days.

proof.awardsLine: Regional Murrow Award · 2× Emmy nominated · #1 post volume across TEGNA — the same engine now points at your salon.

work.title: I engineer short-form to travel
work.intro: Each of these is a real vertical video I hooked, packaged, and pushed until it exploded. Different subjects, but the mechanics that make people stop, watch, and share are exactly what I'll aim at your salon's feed.

items.title: One clip to 1.2M views
items.desc: A single vertical video, engineered hook-to-caption and pushed hard in the first hour.

items.title: 542K plays from one moment
items.desc: Cut and packaged for instant clarity and emotional pull, the exact reason a viewer stops scrolling.

items.title: 374K plays, same day
items.desc: Tight hook, fast turnaround, proof I can move at the speed of a trend and still land the reach.

work.note: Our method is the difference between a post that 200 people scroll past and one that fills a week of appointments. We'll host your transformation right here next.

founder.name: Hi, I'm Makayla.
paragraphs: I'm Makayla Richards. I've spent my career building brands and short-form content at the highest level of media — the same instincts that make videos travel and brands stick. Now I point them at the salon chair.

I take my beauty appointments seriously, and I kept noticing the same thing: the most talented person in the room had the weakest presence online. The braider whose work belongs in a magazine had an overgrown Linktree and a blurry cutout of herself. AI made it easy to throw something together, but not something clients actually want to engage with. That still takes a real eye.

No Empty Chair exists to close that gap, to give the people who are genuinely the best the brand, the site, and the content that finally match their talent, so the right clients find you, trust you, and book you.

steps.title: $100 teardown
steps.desc: I send you a short video breaking down the highest-impact changes to book more clients, and the $100 credits toward another service.

steps.title: We build it
steps.desc: Pick your package and I handle the brand, site, and content, designed to match your vibe and your goals.

steps.title: You get booked
steps.desc: Launch a brand that looks as good as your work, and watch the right clients start finding their way to your chair.

faq.title: The things salon owners ask me

items.q: What does No Empty Chair actually do?
items.a: We're a salon brand and content studio in Atlanta, built for one kind of person — the stylist, braider, or barber whose work is better than their internet. We build the brand, the booking-ready website, and the short-form content that get the right clients to find you and book you.

items.q: How much does a salon website and brand cost?
items.a: Packages run from $1,500 for a focused refresh to $5,500+ for a full rebrand with content. Most salons start with the $100 Glow-Up Teardown — a short video breaking down your highest-impact fixes — and that $100 credits toward whatever you build next.

A short, honest video where I break down what's quietly costing you bookings — your feed, your site, your booking flow — and the three changes that would move the needle first. It's $100, and it credits toward any package.

items.q: Do you only work with salons in Atlanta?
items.a: The studio is in Atlanta and I love a local chair. But websites, branding, and reels travel — if your salon is anywhere and your presence online doesn't match your talent, we can work.

items.q: I already have an Instagram. Why would I need more?
items.a: A feed of good photos isn't a brand, and it isn't a booking system. We turn scattered posts into a look that reads premium, a website that takes the appointment, and reels built to be shared — not just scrolled past.
A Refresh is usually a couple of weeks. A full Statement rebrand runs longer, because we're shooting and building content. You'll have a clear timeline before we start.

items.q: What makes you different from a regular web designer or content person?
items.a: Most people can hand you a pretty page. I spent a career engineering short-form video at the highest level of media — 2× Emmy-nominated, Murrow-winning, top 2% across TEGNA — and I aim that same engine at your chair. Pretty is the floor. Booked is the point.

demos.title: Three salons, three whole worlds
demos.intro: Real, clickable demo sites I built to show the range — a colorful nail studio, a regal hair atelier, a modern braid bar. Yours gets built around you, not dropped into a template.

items.name: POPPI
items.desc: Playful and high-energy, built to turn a scroll into a walk-in.

items.name: Maison Noir
items.desc: Regal and editorial — quiet luxury, by appointment.

items.name: KNOT
items.desc: Clean and trendy — protective styles with honest timing.

contact.title: Let's make your salon the one everyone wants.
contact.sub: Start with a $100 Glow-Up Teardown, a clear, expert look at exactly what's holding your bookings back. It credits toward another service.

radar.headline: Are you on our radar yet?
radar.sub: We're always scouting salons doing incredible work. Drop your details and find out if No Empty Chair already has its eye on you.
radar.successBody: You're officially on our radar. Keep an eye on your inbox — we'll be in touch soon.
